1	\chapter{Relative Picard sheaf --- \texttt{Scheme.Modules.tensorObj} substrate (A.1.c.SubT)}
2	\label{chap:Picard_TensorObjSubstrate}
3	
4	\section{Motivation: the abelian-group-valued relative Picard sheaf}
5	\label{sec:tensorobj_motivation}
6	
7	Fix a base field \(k\) and a smooth proper geometrically integral curve \(C\) over
8	\(k\). In Kleiman's framework (\cref{chap:Picard_RelPicFunctor},
9	\cref{def:rel_pic_sharp}), the relative Picard functor
10	\[
11	  \Pic_{C/k} \;\;\colon\;\; (\Sch/k)^{op} \;\longrightarrow\; \AddCommGrpCat,
12	  \qquad
13	  T \;\longmapsto\; \Pic(C \times_k T) \, / \, \pi_T^* \Pic(T),
14	\]
15	is, by definition, abelian-group-valued. The abelian-group structure on each
16	fiber \(\Pic(C \times_k T)\) comes from tensor product of line bundles
17	(Stacks tag 01CR / Hartshorne~II.\S 6), and the structure descends to the
18	quotient because \(\pi_T^*\) is a group homomorphism.
19	
20	% SOURCE: [Kleiman], "The Picard scheme", \S 2 (FGA Explained Ch.~9 \S 9.2),
21	% Definitions df:aPf + df:Pfs (the absolute and relative Picard functors are
22	% abelian-group-valued; the relative one is defined as a quotient of abelian
23	% groups)
24	% (read from references/kleiman-picard-src/kleiman-picard.tex, L1274-L1318).
25	% SOURCE QUOTE:
26	% "\begin{dfn}\label{df:aPf}
27	%  The {\it absolute Picard functor} $\Pic_X$ is the functor from  the
28	%  category of (locally Noetherian) $S$-schemes $T$ to the category of
29	% abelian groups defined by the formula
30	% 	$$\Pic_X(T):=\Pic(X_T).$$
31	% \end{dfn}
32	%  [\dots]
33	% \begin{dfn}\label{df:Pfs}
34	%   The {\it relative Picard functor} $\Pic_{X/S}$ is defined by
35	% 	$$\Pic_{X/S}(T):=\Pic(X_T)\big/\Pic(T).$$
36	%  Denote its associated sheaves in the Zariski, \'etale, and fppf
37	% topologies  by
38	% 	$$\Pic_{(X/S)\zar},\ \Pic_{(X/S)\et},\
39	% 	\Pic_{(X/S)\fppf}.$$
40	%  \end{dfn}"
41	\textit{Source: [Kleiman], ``The Picard scheme'', \S 2, Defs.~df:aPf + df:Pfs.
42	The absolute and relative Picard functors take values in the category of
43	abelian groups; the relative functor is the quotient of one such group by
44	another, and the structure morphisms of the resulting presheaf are group
45	homomorphisms.}
46	
47	Concretely, the abelian-group law on \(\Pic(C \times_k T)\) is the assignment
48	\(\bigl[\,\mathcal{L}\,\bigr] + \bigl[\,\mathcal{L}'\,\bigr] :=
49	\bigl[\,\mathcal{L} \otimes_{\mathcal{O}_{C \times_k T}} \mathcal{L}'\,\bigr]\),
50	with neutral element the class of the structure sheaf
51	\(\mathcal{O}_{C \times_k T}\) and inverse
52	\(- \bigl[\,\mathcal{L}\,\bigr] := \bigl[\,\mathcal{L}^{-1}\,\bigr]\), where
53	\(\mathcal{L}^{-1} = \mathcal{H}om_{\mathcal{O}_{C \times_k T}}(\mathcal{L},
54	\mathcal{O}_{C \times_k T})\) is the dual invertible sheaf. Pullback of
55	\(\mathcal{O}\)-modules along \(\pi_T : C \times_k T \to T\) preserves tensor
56	products and the structure sheaf, so the image
57	\(\pi_T^* \Pic(T) \subset \Pic(C \times_k T)\) is a subgroup, and the quotient
58	\(\Pic(C \times_k T) / \pi_T^* \Pic(T)\) inherits a canonical abelian-group
59	structure. This is the content of
60	\cref{lem:rel_pic_sharp_groupoid} in chapter \cref{chap:Picard_RelPicFunctor}.
61	
62	The mathematics is straightforward; the obstacle to formalisation is purely
63	infrastructural, and --- crucially --- much smaller than a full
64	monoidal-category construction. The group law lives on \emph{isomorphism
65	classes} of line bundles: each group axiom is a \emph{proposition} asserting
66	the existence of an isomorphism (\(\mathrm{Nonempty}(\,\cdots \cong \cdots)\)),
67	and a proposition is insensitive to which isomorphism witnesses it. In
68	particular the coherence data of a monoidal category --- the pentagon,
69	triangle and hexagon identities, and a fortiori any closed structure --- is
70	\emph{never consumed} by a group law on iso-classes. What the law genuinely
71	requires on the Lean carrier is therefore only:
72	\begin{enumerate}
73	  \item a binary tensor-product operation
74	    \(\otimes\, : \Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X \times \Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X
75	                  \to \Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\), well-defined on
76	    isomorphism classes;
77	  \item the structure sheaf \(\mathcal{O}_X\) as a designated unit object
78	    for \(\otimes\);
79	  \item four existence-of-isomorphism facts on \emph{line bundles}
80	    (locally-trivial rank-one modules): associativity
81	    \((L \otimes M) \otimes N \cong L \otimes (M \otimes N)\), unit
82	    \(\mathcal{O}_X \otimes L \cong L\), commutativity
83	    \(L \otimes M \cong M \otimes L\), and inverse
84	    \(L \otimes L^{-1} \cong \mathcal{O}_X\) with
85	    \(L^{-1} = \mathcal{H}om(L, \mathcal{O}_X)\).
86	\end{enumerate}
87	At Mathlib's pinned commit, only a presheaf-level version of (1) is
88	available; \(\mathcal{O}_X\) and the dual are present as scheme-level
89	objects. The four facts of (3) all reduce to a single non-trivial
90	ingredient --- the \emph{global} compatibility isomorphism
91	\(\mathtt{tensorObj\_restrict\_iso}\) (\cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso})
92	expressing that \(\otimes_X\) commutes with restriction to an open. Local
93	triviality alone does \emph{not} furnish a global associator: two sheaves
94	each locally isomorphic to \(\mathcal{O}\) need not be globally isomorphic
95	(\(\mathcal{O}(1)\) versus \(\mathcal{O}\) on \(\mathbb{P}^1\)). The global
96	compatibility isomorphism is nonetheless available for \emph{arbitrary}
97	modules \(M, N\), because the relevant restriction is along an \emph{open
98	immersion}: restriction is then computed sectionwise, and the comparison of
99	structure-sheaf rings over the image is the \emph{isomorphism}
100	\(f.\mathtt{appIso}\), so the base change is extension of scalars along a ring
101	isomorphism --- which is automatically an isomorphism and commutes with
102	\(\otimes\). No line-bundle or flatness hypothesis is needed. This is
103	exactly the carrier on which the relative Picard group law of
104	\cref{lem:rel_pic_sharp_groupoid} rests, and it is the subject of
105	\cref{sec:tensorobj_substrate,sec:tensorobj_onproduct_lift} below. The full symmetric monoidal category on
106	\emph{all} quasi-coherent modules is \emph{not} required; see
107	\cref{rem:scheme_modules_monoidal_off_path}.
108	
109	\section{Mathlib API survey}
110	\label{sec:tensorobj_api_survey}
111	
112	The relevant pieces of the Mathlib API at the pinned commit
113	(\texttt{b80f227}) split across two layers.
114	
115	\paragraph{Layer 1: presheaf-of-modules monoidal structure.} Mathlib's
116	\texttt{Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Monoidal.PresheafOfModules} module
117	constructs a monoidal-category structure on the category
118	\(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules}\,R\) of presheaves of modules over a presheaf of
119	commutative rings \(R\); in particular, it exposes a tensor-product
120	operation \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj}\) realising the
121	sectionwise tensor product
122	\[
123	  (M \otimes_R N)(U) \;\;=\;\; M(U) \otimes_{R(U)} N(U)
124	\]
125	for open sets / objects \(U\) in the indexing site, together with the
126	associator, left/right unitors, and braiding making the resulting
127	category symmetric monoidal. This is the presheaf-level analogue of
128	Stacks tag 03DM (relative tensor product of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules).
129	
130	\paragraph{Layer 2: scheme-level \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules.} The module
131	\(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\) on a scheme \(X\) (the Lean target
132	expressed by the namespace \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules})
133	realises the category of sheaves of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules
134	on the small Zariski site of \(X\). It comes with pullback functoriality
135	(\(\mathtt{Scheme.Modules.pullback}\), used heavily in
136	\cref{chap:Picard_LineBundlePullback}), pullback comparison isomorphisms
137	(\(\mathtt{Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp}\),
138	\(\mathtt{Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr}\)), and the structure-sheaf
139	constant module \(\mathcal{O}_X\). It does \emph{not} ship a binary
140	tensor-product operation
141	\(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X \times \Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X \to
142	\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\), nor a \(\MonoidalCategory\) instance.
143	
144	\paragraph{The gap.} The substrate \(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}.\mathtt{tensorObj}\)
145	this chapter introduces is the named operation lifting
146	\(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj}\) from Layer 1 into
147	Layer 2, packaged so that the consumer at chapter
148	\cref{chap:Picard_RelPicFunctor} can read it as a binary operation on
149	\(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}(C \times_k T)\). The \emph{single} genuinely
150	non-trivial fact about this operation that the group law needs is the
151	\emph{restriction-compatibility isomorphism}
152	\(\mathtt{tensorObj\_restrict\_iso}\) (\cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso}):
153	for \emph{arbitrary} modules \(M, N\) and the restriction along an open immersion
154	\(f\), the canonical comparison
155	\((M \otimes_X N)|_f \cong M|_f \otimes N|_f\) is an isomorphism. This is
156	the \emph{only} input the four existence-of-iso group-law lemmas of
157	\cref{sec:tensorobj_onproduct_lift} consume. Because \(f\) is an open immersion,
158	restriction is sectionwise and the structure-sheaf comparison is the ring
159	isomorphism \(f.\mathtt{appIso}\); the comparison is then base change along that
160	ring isomorphism, an isomorphism commuting with \(\otimes\) by
161	\(\mathtt{ModuleCat.restrictScalarsEquivalenceOfRingEquiv}\). The sole
162	project-side ingredient is a bounded sectionwise unfolding of
163	\(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules.pullback}\,\varphi\) along the open immersion; no
164	flatness and no line-bundle hypothesis enter (\cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso}).
165	The project does \emph{not} require a global \(\MonoidalCategory\) instance
166	on all quasi-coherent \(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\), nor a
167	\(\mathtt{MonoidalClosed}\) structure: monoidal coherence is never consumed
168	by a group law on iso-classes (\cref{rem:scheme_modules_monoidal_off_path}).
169	The project's policy on this gap is \emph{project-side}: the substrate is
170	built inside \texttt{AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/} (the file
171	\(\texttt{AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/TensorObjSubstrate.lean}\) is its home)
172	and the consumer instance is closed against it. A parallel Mathlib upstream
173	PR with the same content is welcome but not on the critical path for the
174	project; the consumer is closed against the project-side substrate
175	independently of any upstream timing.
176	
177	\section{The substrate \(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}.\mathtt{tensorObj}\)}
178	\label{sec:tensorobj_substrate}
179	
180	\begin{definition}
181	
182	\leanok
183	[The substrate operation $\otimes$ on $\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X$]
184	  \label{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj}
185	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorObj}
186	  Let \(X\) be a scheme. For
187	  \(M, N \in \Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\), define
188	  \[
189	    M \otimes_X N \;\;\in\;\; \Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X
190	  \]
191	  as follows. Choose an affine open cover \(\{U_i = \Spec A_i\}_{i \in I}\)
192	  of \(X\). On each \(U_i\), the restriction
193	  \(M|_{U_i}\) corresponds (via the equivalence between
194	  \(\mathcal{O}_{\Spec A_i}\)-modules and \(A_i\)-modules) to an
195	  \(A_i\)-module \(M_i\), and similarly
196	  \(N|_{U_i}\) corresponds to an \(A_i\)-module \(N_i\). Define
197	  \((M \otimes_X N)|_{U_i}\) as the sheafification of the presheaf
198	  \(U_i \mapsto M_i \otimes_{A_i} N_i\). On overlaps
199	  \(U_i \cap U_j = \Spec A_{ij}\) the resulting modules agree via the
200	  canonical compatibility
201	  \((M_i \otimes_{A_i} N_i) \otimes_{A_i} A_{ij}
202	     \;\cong\;
203	     (M_j \otimes_{A_j} N_j) \otimes_{A_j} A_{ij}\),
204	  so the data \(\{(M \otimes_X N)|_{U_i}\}\) glue to a single object
205	  \(M \otimes_X N\) in \(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\). The result is
206	  canonically independent of the affine cover chosen.
207	
208	  Equivalently: the underlying presheaf of \(M \otimes_X N\) is the
209	  presheaf-of-modules tensor product
210	  \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj}\) of the underlying
211	  presheaves of \(M\) and \(N\), composed with the sheafification
212	  functor on the small Zariski site of \(X\). The substrate operation
213	  \(\otimes_X\) is, by construction, a \(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\)
214	  object reflecting the relative tensor product of
215	  \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules described in Stacks tag 03DM.
216	\end{definition}
217	
218	\begin{lemma}
219	
220	\leanok
221	[Functoriality of $\otimes_X$]
222	  \label{lem:scheme_modules_tensorobj_functoriality}
223	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorObj_functoriality}
224	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj}
225	  Let \(X\) be a scheme. The assignment
226	  \((M, N) \mapsto M \otimes_X N\) of
227	  \cref{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj} extends to a bifunctor
228	  \[
229	    \otimes_X \;\;\colon\;\; \Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X \;\times\;
230	                          \Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X \;\;\longrightarrow\;\;
231	                          \Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X
232	  \]
233	  natural in both arguments: a pair of morphisms
234	  \(f : M \to M'\) and \(g : N \to N'\) determines a morphism
235	  \(f \otimes g : M \otimes_X N \to M' \otimes_X N'\) compatible with
236	  identities and composition. This bifunctorial action on morphisms is
237	  the only datum the present lemma provides. The further coherence data
238	  of a monoidal structure --- the unitors
239	  \[
240	    \lambda_M \;\colon\; \mathcal{O}_X \otimes_X M \;\xrightarrow{\sim}\; M,
241	    \qquad
242	    \rho_M \;\colon\; M \otimes_X \mathcal{O}_X \;\xrightarrow{\sim}\; M,
243	  \]
244	  the associator
245	  \(\alpha_{M, N, P} : (M \otimes_X N) \otimes_X P \xrightarrow{\sim}
246	   M \otimes_X (N \otimes_X P)\), and the braiding
247	  \(\beta_{M, N} : M \otimes_X N \xrightarrow{\sim} N \otimes_X M\) ---
248	  are \emph{not} outputs of this lemma; they have no current Lean
249	  declaration and are off the critical path, recorded in
250	  \cref{rem:scheme_modules_monoidal_off_path}.
251	\end{lemma}
252	
253	\begin{proof}
254	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj}
255	  Pick an affine open cover \(\{U_i = \Spec A_i\}\) of \(X\). On each
256	  affine, the assignment is the standard tensor product of modules over a
257	  commutative ring \(A_i\), which is bifunctorial. The bifunctorial action
258	  on morphisms glues across the cover because the underlying gluing data of
259	  \cref{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj} respect the canonical morphisms on
260	  overlaps; equivalently, the morphism action is inherited from
261	  \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj}\) (Mathlib module
262	  \texttt{Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Monoidal.PresheafOfModules}) under
263	  sheafification. The unitor/associator/braiding coherence data are
264	  \emph{not} constructed here; they are off the critical path
265	  (\cref{rem:scheme_modules_monoidal_off_path}).
266	\end{proof}
267	
268	\begin{remark}
269	[The full monoidal category on all modules is off the critical path]
270	  \label{rem:scheme_modules_monoidal_off_path}
271	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj, lem:scheme_modules_tensorobj_functoriality}
272	  One could ask for the bifunctor \(\otimes_X\) of
273	  \cref{lem:scheme_modules_tensorobj_functoriality}, together with
274	  \(\mathcal{O}_X\), \(\alpha\), \(\lambda\), \(\rho\) and \(\beta\), to
275	  assemble into a full symmetric \(\MonoidalCategory\) structure on
276	  \emph{all} of \(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\), discharging the pentagon,
277	  triangle and hexagon coherence identities. This chapter deliberately does
278	  \emph{not} pursue that, and the relative Picard group law does \emph{not}
279	  depend on it.
280	
281	  The reason is that the relative Picard group law of
282	  \cref{lem:rel_pic_sharp_groupoid} is a structure on \emph{isomorphism
283	  classes} of line bundles, and every group axiom there is a
284	  \emph{proposition} of the form \(\mathrm{Nonempty}(\cdots \cong \cdots)\).
285	  Such a proposition is insensitive to which isomorphism witnesses it, so the
286	  coherence data of a monoidal category --- pentagon, triangle, hexagon, and
287	  a fortiori any monoidal-closed structure --- is never consumed by the group
288	  law. Conversely, building the global \(\MonoidalCategory\) on all
289	  quasi-coherent modules would route through the localization-monoidal
290	  transport \(\mathtt{CategoryTheory.Localization.Monoidal}\), which demands a
291	  \(\mathtt{MorphismProperty.IsMonoidal}\,W\) instance for the
292	  sheafification-localizing morphism property \(W\); the
293	  \(\mathtt{whiskerLeft}\) stability \(W\,g \Rightarrow W(F \triangleleft g)\)
294	  of that property, for an \emph{arbitrary} module \(F\), is provable only
295	  through a \(\mathtt{MonoidalClosed}(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules}\,R)\) ---
296	  absent from Mathlib at the pinned commit, and a multi-file build. That cost
297	  buys coherence the group law never uses.
298	
299	  The group law needs only the four existence-of-iso lemmas of
300	  \cref{sec:tensorobj_onproduct_lift}, each assembled from the
301	  restriction-compatibility isomorphism \cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso}. We
302	  therefore record the global monoidal category as explicitly \emph{off the
303	  critical path} and build the group law directly on the line-bundle
304	  subcategory. This mirrors Mathlib's own Picard group
305	  \(\mathtt{CommRing.Pic}\,R\) (\texttt{Mathlib.RingTheory.PicardGroup}),
306	  whose \(\mathtt{CommGroup}\) is transported from
307	  \(\mathrm{Units}\bigl(\mathrm{Skeleton}(\mathtt{ModuleCat}\,R)\bigr)\) over
308	  a fixed ring and needs no sheafification, no localization, and no
309	  \(\mathtt{MonoidalClosed}\).
310	\end{remark}
311	
312	\section{The lift through \(\texttt{LineBundle.OnProduct}\)}
313	\label{sec:tensorobj_onproduct_lift}
314	
315	The consumer in chapter \cref{chap:Picard_RelPicFunctor} works not with
316	\(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}(C \times_k T)\) directly but with the subtype
317	\(\texttt{LineBundle.OnProduct}\,\pi_C\,\pi_T\) of locally-trivial
318	modules on the fiber product \(C \times_k T\), introduced in
319	\cref{chap:Picard_LineBundlePullback}. This section first isolates the one
320	genuinely-hard ingredient --- the restriction-compatibility
321	isomorphism \cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso} --- and then records the
322	\emph{four existence-of-isomorphism facts on line bundles} (associativity,
323	unit, commutativity and inverse) that the relative Picard group law
324	consumes, each assembled from that ingredient on a common trivialising
325	cover, together with their restriction to the
326	\(\texttt{LineBundle.OnProduct}\) subtype and the interaction with the
327	pullback functor \(\pi_T^*\). These four facts (\emph{not} a
328	\(\MonoidalCategory\) instance) are exactly the group-law data; cf.\
329	\cref{rem:scheme_modules_monoidal_off_path}.
330	
331	\begin{lemma}
332	[Sectionwise lax-monoidal structure on \(\mathtt{restrictScalars}\,\varphi\)]
333	  \label{lem:restrictscalars_laxmonoidal}
334	  \lean{PresheafOfModules.restrictScalarsLaxMonoidal}
335	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj}
336	  % NOTE: this CommRingCat-level lax-monoidal instance
337	  % (`PresheafOfModules.restrictScalarsLaxMonoidal`, with helpers
338	  % `restrictScalarsLaxε`, `restrictScalarsLaxμ`) is a self-contained
339	  % supplement. It is NOT used by `lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso`, whose proof
340	  % (open-immersion sectionwise base change) identifies restriction along an
341	  % open immersion with base change along a structure-sheaf isomorphism and
342	  % needs no lax-monoidal pushforward. Retained for potential reuse; off the
343	  % critical path for the tensor group law.
344	  Let \(\varphi : R \to S\) be a morphism of presheaves of \emph{commutative}
345	  rings on a site (so that both \(R\) and \(S\) factor through
346	  \(\mathtt{CommRingCat}\) and the category of presheaves of modules over each
347	  is monoidal). Then the presheaf-of-modules restriction-of-scalars functor
348	  \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars}\,\varphi\) is lax monoidal.
349	  Consequently, composing with the already-monoidal Mathlib functor
350	  \(\mathtt{pushforward}_0\mathtt{OfCommRingCat}\) via
351	  \(\mathtt{Functor.LaxMonoidal.comp}\), the full pushforward
352	  \[
353	    \mathtt{pushforward}\,\varphi
354	      \;\;:=\;\;
355	    \mathtt{pushforward}_0\,F\,R \;\circ\; \mathtt{restrictScalars}\,\varphi
356	  \]
357	  is lax monoidal. This lemma is a self-contained
358	  \(\mathtt{CommRingCat}\)-level lax-monoidal supplement: it stands on its own
359	  and is retained for potential reuse, but it is \emph{off the critical path}
360	  for the tensor group law. In particular it is \emph{not} an ingredient of
361	  \cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso}, whose proof identifies restriction along an
362	  open immersion with base change along a structure-sheaf isomorphism and
363	  consequently needs no lax-monoidal structure on the pushforward.
364	\end{lemma}
365	
366	\begin{proof}
367	  This is a \emph{sectionwise} lift of the existing fact that, for a ring map
368	  \(f : R \to S\), the module restriction-of-scalars functor
369	  \(\mathtt{ModuleCat.restrictScalars}\,f\) is lax monoidal
370	  (\texttt{Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Monoidal.Adjunction}, where it is
371	  obtained as the \(\mathtt{rightAdjointLaxMonoidal}\) of the
372	  extension/restriction adjunction \(\mathtt{extendRestrictScalarsAdj}\) ---
373	  i.e.\ Mathlib already uses the same mate idiom one level down). The presheaf
374	  monoidal structure is computed sectionwise,
375	  \((M_1 \otimes M_2).\mathrm{obj}\,X = M_1.\mathrm{obj}\,X \otimes M_2.\mathrm{obj}\,X\),
376	  and the presheaf functor \(\mathtt{restrictScalars}\,\varphi\) acts
377	  sectionwise as \(\mathtt{ModuleCat.restrictScalars}\,(\varphi.\mathrm{app}\,X)\).
378	  Hence the lax structure maps \(\mu\) (laxity) and \(\varepsilon\) (unit)
379	  assemble from the per-section \(\mathtt{ModuleCat}\) ones, the required
380	  naturality squares being the naturality of the sectionwise maps against the
381	  presheaf restriction morphisms. Packaging these as a
382	  \(\mathtt{Functor.CoreLaxMonoidal}\) yields the lax-monoidal structure; no
383	  new diagram chase is needed beyond transporting the sectionwise coherence.
384	  The composite \(\mathtt{pushforward}\,\varphi\) is then lax monoidal by
385	  \(\mathtt{Functor.LaxMonoidal.comp}\) together with the monoidality of
386	  \(\mathtt{pushforward}_0\mathtt{OfCommRingCat}\) (Mathlib), modulo a defeq
387	  transport across the definitional unfolding of \(\mathtt{pushforward}\,\varphi\).
388	
389	  The commutativity hypothesis is essential: the presheaf-of-modules monoidal
390	  category requires \(\mathtt{CommRingCat}\)-valued presheaves of rings, so the
391	  instance must be stated over \(\mathtt{CommRingCat}\)-factored \(R, S\). In
392	  the project this holds: the relevant \(\varphi\) is
393	  \((\mathtt{Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom}\,f).\mathrm{hom}\), which is
394	  structure-sheaf-valued and hence commutative-ring-valued.
395	\end{proof}
396	
397	\begin{lemma}
398	
399	\leanok
400	[Tensor product commutes with restriction along an open immersion]
401	  \label{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso}
402	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorObj_restrict_iso}
403	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj}
404	  Let \(X\) be a scheme, let \(M, N \in \Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\) be
405	  \emph{arbitrary} \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules, and let
406	  \(f : U \hookrightarrow X\) be an open immersion, with \((-)|_f\) the
407	  restriction of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules along \(f\). The canonical comparison
408	  morphism
409	  \[
410	    (M \otimes_X N)\big|_f
411	      \;\xrightarrow{\;\;}\;
412	    M\big|_f \;\otimes_U\; N\big|_f
413	  \]
414	  is an isomorphism. No local-freeness, flatness, or line-bundle hypothesis on
415	  \(M, N\) is required: the statement holds for all \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules.
416	  The reason is structural to open immersions --- restriction along \(f\) is
417	  base change along the structure-sheaf \emph{isomorphism} induced by \(f\),
418	  not along a general ring map --- and base change along a ring isomorphism is
419	  trivially invertible and commutes with tensor products. The intended Lean
420	  target is
421	  \(\texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorObj\_restrict\_iso}\).
422	\end{lemma}
423	
424	\begin{proof}
425	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj}
426	  Write \(\varphi\) for the morphism of structure-sheaf-valued presheaves of
427	  rings induced by the open immersion \(f\) (concretely
428	  \(\varphi = (\mathtt{Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom}\,f).\mathrm{hom}\)), so that
429	  restriction along \(f\) is computed by the inverse-image (pullback) functor on
430	  presheaves of modules. The proof exploits the defining feature of an open
431	  immersion: the comparison of structure-sheaf rings over the image is an
432	  \emph{isomorphism}, not merely a ring map. For an open \(V \subseteq U\) the
433	  section ring \(\Gamma\bigl(X, f(V)\bigr)\) of \(\mathcal{O}_X\) over the image
434	  of \(V\) is identified with \(\Gamma(U, V)\) by the canonical \emph{ring
435	  isomorphism}
436	  \(f.\mathtt{appIso}\,V : \Gamma\bigl(X, f(V)\bigr) \xrightarrow{\sim}
437	  \Gamma(U, V)\)
438	  (the \(\mathtt{CommRingCat}\) isomorphism
439	  \(\mathtt{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Hom.appIso}\)). Restriction along \(f\) is
440	  therefore extension of scalars along
441	  this ring isomorphism, and the proof needs no flatness anywhere.
442	
443	  \emph{Step 1 (reduce restriction to the abstract pullback).} The restriction
444	  \((-)|_f\) of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules along the open immersion \(f\) is
445	  naturally isomorphic to the abstract presheaf-of-modules pullback
446	  \((\mathtt{PresheafOfModules.pullback}\,\varphi).\mathrm{obj}(-)\), via the
447	  present comparison \(\mathtt{Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctorIsoPullback}\). It
448	  therefore suffices to produce the comparison and prove it an isomorphism for
449	  the abstract pullback functor.
450	
451	  \emph{Step 2 (move the pullback inside the sheafification).} The substrate
452	  \(\otimes_X\) is sheafification of the presheaf-of-modules tensor product
453	  (\cref{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj}). The pullback commutes with
454	  sheafification: there is a natural isomorphism
455	  \(\mathtt{sheafification} \circ \mathtt{pullback}\,\varphi
456	   \;\cong\;
457	   \mathtt{PresheafOfModules.pullback}\,\varphi \circ \mathtt{sheafification}\)
458	  (the present \(\mathtt{SheafOfModules.sheafificationCompPullback}\)). Hence the
459	  whole question descends to the presheaf level, where it is the base-change
460	  comparison
461	  \[
462	    (\mathtt{pullback}\,\varphi).\mathrm{obj}(A \otimes B)
463	      \;\longrightarrow\;
464	    (\mathtt{pullback}\,\varphi).\mathrm{obj}\,A
465	      \;\otimes\;
466	    (\mathtt{pullback}\,\varphi).\mathrm{obj}\,B
467	  \]
468	  for the underlying presheaves of modules \(A, B\) of \(M\) and \(N\).
469	
470	  \emph{Step 3 (sectionwise identification along the open-immersion ring
471	  isomorphism).} The functor \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules.pullback}\,\varphi\) is
472	  \emph{defined} as the left adjoint of pushforward and is consequently
473	  \emph{opaque} on objects and morphisms: Mathlib provides no sectionwise
474	  formula for it, and --- unlike the scheme-level restriction --- no
475	  presheaf-level analogue of \(\mathtt{restrictFunctorIsoPullback}\). The one
476	  genuinely-new project-side ingredient of this proof is therefore a
477	  \emph{sectionwise unfolding} of the abstract pullback along the open
478	  immersion: for the open-immersion ring morphism \(\varphi\), the presheaf
479	  \((\mathtt{pullback}\,\varphi).\mathrm{obj}\,P\) is, section by section over an
480	  open \(V\), the base change of the sections of \(P\) along the ring
481	  \emph{isomorphism} \(f.\mathtt{appIso}\,V\) --- that is, restriction of
482	  sections combined with scalar transport across the isomorphism. (Over an open
483	  immersion the sections themselves agree definitionally,
484	  \(\Gamma(M|_f, V) = \Gamma(M, f(V))\) by the \(\mathtt{restrict\_obj}\)
485	  identity; only the scalar ring is transported, and it is transported along an
486	  isomorphism.)
487	
488	  Granting this sectionwise description, the base-change comparison of Step 2
489	  is, on each section over \(V\), the canonical map of a tensor product under
490	  base change along the ring isomorphism \(f.\mathtt{appIso}\,V\). Base change
491	  along a ring isomorphism is a \emph{monoidal equivalence} of module
492	  categories --- it is invertible and carries tensor products to tensor products
493	  --- by \(\mathtt{ModuleCat.restrictScalarsEquivalenceOfRingEquiv}\) applied to
494	  the ring equivalence underlying \(f.\mathtt{appIso}\,V\). Hence each
495	  sectionwise comparison is an isomorphism compatible with \(\otimes\). No
496	  flatness, no \(\mathtt{extendScalars}\)-monoidality, and no strong-monoidal
497	  pullback instance is invoked: the entire force of the step is that the scalar
498	  change is along an \emph{isomorphism}.
499	
500	  \emph{Gluing.} The sectionwise isomorphisms of Step 3 are natural in \(V\)
501	  (compatible with the presheaf restriction maps, the naturality square being
502	  that of \(f.\mathtt{appIso}\) against restriction). They therefore assemble,
503	  via \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules.isoMk}\), into a single presheaf-of-modules
504	  isomorphism realising the base-change comparison of Step 2 as an iso.
505	  Transporting this back through the sheafification units of Step 2 and the
506	  restriction--pullback comparison of Step 1 yields the asserted global
507	  isomorphism \((M \otimes_X N)|_f \cong M|_f \otimes_U N|_f\).
508	
509	  The route uses no monoidal structure on \(\mathtt{SheafOfModules}\) or on the
510	  pullback functor, no flatness, and no line-bundle hypothesis. The single
511	  bounded project-side ingredient is the sectionwise unfolding of
512	  \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules.pullback}\,\varphi\) along an open immersion (a
513	  short helper of roughly \(30\)--\(60\) lines, mirroring one layer down the
514	  scheme-level \(\mathtt{restrictFunctorIsoPullback}\) and with direct precedent
515	  in the analogous presheaf-pullback unfolding used for the base change of
516	  Kähler differentials); every other ingredient
517	  (\(\mathtt{restrictFunctorIsoPullback}\),
518	  \(\mathtt{sheafificationCompPullback}\),
519	  \(\mathtt{restrictScalarsEquivalenceOfRingEquiv}\),

526	[Tensor product of locally-trivial modules is locally trivial]
527	  \label{lem:tensorobj_preserves_locally_trivial}
528	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorObj_isLocallyTrivial}
529	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj}
530	  Let \(X\) be a scheme, and let
531	  \(L, L' \in \Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\) be locally-trivial modules of
532	  rank one (i.e.\ line bundles). The tensor product \(L \otimes_X L'\) of
533	  \cref{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj} is again locally trivial of rank
534	  one. Consequently the bifunctor \(\otimes_X\) restricts to a bifunctor
535	  on the full subcategory \(\texttt{LineBundle}\,X \subset \Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\)
536	  of locally-trivial rank-one modules.
537	\end{lemma}
538	
539	\begin{proof}
540	  Pick a common affine open cover \(\{U_i\}\) of \(X\) on which both
541	  \(L|_{U_i}\) and \(L'|_{U_i}\) are free of rank one. On each \(U_i\)
542	  there are trivialisations \(L|_{U_i} \cong \mathcal{O}_{U_i}\) and
543	  \(L'|_{U_i} \cong \mathcal{O}_{U_i}\). The tensor product of two free
544	  rank-one modules over a commutative ring is again free of rank one;
545	  hence \((L \otimes_X L')|_{U_i} \cong \mathcal{O}_{U_i}\). So
546	  \(L \otimes_X L'\) is locally trivial of rank one, i.e.\ an object of
547	  \(\texttt{LineBundle}\,X\).
548	\end{proof}

551	[Associativity of $\otimes_X$ on line bundles]
552	  \label{lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso}
553	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj, lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso,
554	        lem:tensorobj_preserves_locally_trivial}
555	  % NOTE: no Lean declaration yet; blocked on lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso.

557	  bundles. Then there exists an isomorphism
558	  \[
559	    (L \otimes_X M) \otimes_X N
560	      \;\cong\;
561	    L \otimes_X (M \otimes_X N)
562	  \]
563	  in \(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\); equivalently the proposition
564	  \(\mathrm{Nonempty}\bigl((L \otimes_X M) \otimes_X N \cong
565	  L \otimes_X (M \otimes_X N)\bigr)\) holds.
566	\end{lemma}
567	
568	\begin{proof}
569	  \uses{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso, lem:tensorobj_preserves_locally_trivial}
570	  Choose a common affine open cover \(\{U_i = \Spec A_i\}\) of \(X\)
571	  trivialising \(L, M, N\) simultaneously. By
572	  \cref{lem:tensorobj_preserves_locally_trivial} every tensor product
573	  occurring on either side is again a line bundle;
574	  \cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso} (which holds for arbitrary
575	  \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules) applies and both sides restrict to the
576	  iterated tensor product of \(A_i\)-modules on \(U_i\). There the associator
577	  \((L_i \otimes_{A_i} M_i) \otimes_{A_i} N_i \cong
578	  L_i \otimes_{A_i} (M_i \otimes_{A_i} N_i)\) is the standard associativity
579	  isomorphism of the tensor product of modules over a commutative ring. These
580	  local isomorphisms agree on overlaps --- naturality of the module
581	  associator against the restriction-comparison isomorphisms of
582	  \cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso} --- and therefore glue to a global
583	  isomorphism. Only the \emph{existence} of the global isomorphism is
584	  asserted; no compatibility with a pentagon identity is claimed or needed.
585	\end{proof}

641	  \]
642	  is again a line bundle on \(X\), and there is a canonical natural
643	  isomorphism
644	  \(\varepsilon_L : L \otimes_X L^{-1} \xrightarrow{\sim} \mathcal{O}_X\).
645	  Consequently every line bundle has a two-sided tensor inverse under
646	  \(\otimes_X\), and the monoid \(\bigl(\texttt{LineBundle}\,X / \sim, \otimes_X,
647	  \mathcal{O}_X\bigr)\) of isomorphism classes is an abelian group ---
648	  the Picard group \(\Pic(X)\) (Stacks tag 01CR).

654	  \(\mathcal{H}om(-, \mathcal{O}_X)\): on a common affine trivialising
655	  cover, the dual of a free rank-one module is again free of rank one.
656	  The contraction map

658	  morphism in \(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\), and on each open of the
659	  trivialising cover it specialises to the contraction
660	  \(A \otimes_A A \to A\) of free rank-one \(A\)-modules, which is an
661	  isomorphism; hence the contraction is a global isomorphism. The
662	  abelian-group structure on \(\Pic(X) := \texttt{LineBundle}\,X / \sim\)

727	  \label{lem:pullback_compatible_with_tensorobj}
728	  \uses{lem:tensorobj_lift_onproduct}

731	  functor

734	                          \texttt{LineBundle.OnProduct}\,\pi_C\,\pi_T
735	  \]
736	  of \cref{def:pullback_along_projection} is a (strong) monoidal
737	  functor: there is a canonical natural isomorphism
738	  \(\pi_T^*(\mathcal{N} \otimes_T \mathcal{N'})
739	     \xrightarrow{\sim}
740	   \pi_T^* \mathcal{N} \otimes_{C \times_S T} \pi_T^* \mathcal{N'}\)
741	  for \(\mathcal{N}, \mathcal{N'} \in \texttt{LineBundle}\,T\), and a
742	  canonical isomorphism

744	  both inherited from the analogous data on the underlying
745	  \(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\) functors via the

754	  \(B \otimes_A (M \otimes_A M')
755	   \;\cong\; (B \otimes_A M) \otimes_B (B \otimes_A M')\)
756	  for a ring map \(A \to B\) and \(A\)-modules \(M, M'\). This affine
757	  identity glues across an affine trivialising cover of \(T\): on each
758	  affine open where \(\mathcal{N}\) and \(\mathcal{N}'\) are free of rank one

1	# Analogy: bounded re-route for `tensorObj_restrict_iso` (open-immersion vs line-bundle vs the dead δ-route)
2	
3	## Mode
4	api-alignment
5	
6	## Slug
7	tsroute208
8	
9	## Iteration
10	208
11	
12	## Question
13	Lane TS's abstract-adjoint comparison-map (mate δ) route for
14	`tensorObj_restrict_iso` is definitively blocked (it needs the absent
15	`(PresheafOfModules.pullback φ).Monoidal`). Which re-route is the
16	Mathlib-idiomatic, *bounded* one — (A) exploit that `f` is an open immersion
17	(sectionwise restriction + base-change-along-a-ring-iso), or (B) add
18	`IsLocallyTrivial` hypotheses and glue local isos — and do its prerequisites
19	exist at `b80f227` or are they an absent multi-file build?
20	
21	## Project artifact(s)
22	- `AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/TensorObjSubstrate.lean:330-367` — `tensorObj_restrict_iso`
23	  (current body: 2 real reduction steps + residual `sorry`).
24	- `AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/TensorObjSubstrate.lean:105-178` — the iter-207
25	  `restrictScalarsLaxMonoidal` instance built for the now-dead δ-route.
26	- `informal/tensorObj_restrict_iso.md`, `analogies/mate207.md`,
27	  `analogies/ts-design206.md`, `analogies/kaehler-tensorequiv-presheafpullback.md`.
28	
29	## Key mathematical fact the routes hinge on
30	
31	`tensorObj_restrict_iso` is **TRUE for arbitrary `M N : X.Modules`** — tensor
32	product commutes with restriction to an open subscheme with *no* local-freeness
33	hypothesis. Restriction along an open immersion is exact and is base change
34	along the structure-sheaf **isomorphism** `f.appIso`, not a general flat ring
35	map. This single observation reshapes the whole decision: Route (B)'s
36	`IsLocallyTrivial` hypotheses are mathematically *unnecessary* for this lemma,
37	and the δ-route's `extendScalars`-monoidality (needed only because it modeled
38	restriction as a *general* base change) is overkill.
39	
40	## Decisions identified
41	
42	### Decision A: Route (A) — open-immersion sectionwise / base-change-along-a-ring-iso
43	
44	- **Mathlib idiom**: restriction along an open immersion is the **concrete,
45	  definitionally sectionwise** functor `Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctor`, NOT the
46	  abstract pullback. The load-bearing facts are all present at `b80f227`:
47	  - `restrict_obj` / `restrict_map` (`Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/Modules/Sheaf.lean:328,331`)
48	    — **both `rfl`**: `Γ(M.restrict f, U) = Γ(M, f ''ᵁ U)`. Restriction = reindex
49	    sections along `f.opensFunctor`.
50	  - `Scheme.Hom.appIso (U) : Γ(Y, f ''ᵁ U) ≅ Γ(X, U)`
51	    (`Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/OpenImmersion.lean:190`) — the `CommRingCat`
52	    isomorphism along which `restrictFunctor` reindexes scalars (it is literally
53	    `α.app U := (f.appIso U.unop).inv` in `restrictFunctor`'s def, Sheaf.lean:320).
54	  - `ModuleCat.restrictScalarsEquivalenceOfRingEquiv (e : R ≃+* S)`
55	    (`Mathlib/Algebra/Category/ModuleCat/ChangeOfRings.lean:285`) — base change
56	    along a **ring iso** is an equivalence (the "extension of scalars along an
57	    iso is trivially iso" the directive asks for). PRESENT.
58	  - `PresheafOfModules.isoMk` (`Mathlib/Algebra/Category/ModuleCat/Presheaf.lean:118`)
59	    — assemble a sectionwise `app + naturality` into a presheaf-of-modules iso.
60	  - Iso-checking primitives: `Scheme.Modules.Hom.isIso_iff_isIso_app`
61	    (Sheaf.lean:132) — `IsIso φ ↔ ∀ U, IsIso (φ.app U)`; plus the stalkwise
62	    fallback `TopCat.Presheaf.isIso_iff_stalkFunctor_map_iso` (Stalks.lean:652)
63	    and `Scheme.Modules.restrictStalkNatIso` (Sheaf.lean:425, restriction
64	    commutes with stalks).
65	- **The ONE genuine gap (bounded)**: to commute `.restrict f` past the
66	  `sheafification` inside `tensorObj`, the only Mathlib lemma is
67	  `SheafOfModules.sheafificationCompPullback` (PullbackContinuous.lean:117),
68	  which lands you on the **abstract** `PresheafOfModules.pullback φ.hom`
69	  (`Presheaf/Pullback.lean:44`, defined as `(pushforward φ).leftAdjoint` — no
70	  sectionwise formula). Mathlib has **no sectionwise unfolding** of presheaf
71	  `pullback`, and **no presheaf-level analogue of `restrictFunctorIsoPullback`**
72	  (i.e. "pullback along an open immersion ≅ sectionwise restriction" at the
73	  presheaf layer). This must be supplied project-side. **But it is bounded**:
74	  `analogies/kaehler-tensorequiv-presheafpullback.md` (Decision 5) hit the exact
75	  same opacity and estimated a **~30–60 LOC sectionwise-unfolding helper**, and
76	  that lane successfully built a base-change-via-`PresheafOfModules.pullback`
77	  iso. Here it is *easier* because `φ = (toRingCatSheafHom f).hom` is an
78	  open-immersion `appIso` (an iso), so the unfolded base change is along a ring
79	  iso (`restrictScalarsEquivalenceOfRingEquiv`), not a general algebra map.
80	- **Gap**: NEEDS_MATHLIB_GAP_FILL — but **small and single-file** (one
81	  presheaf-pullback-along-open-immersion sectionwise identification), NOT the
82	  multi-file monoidal build the δ-route needs. No new monoidal/`extendScalars`
83	  infra.
84	- **Verdict**: **PROCEED** with Route (A) (after landing the bounded unfolding
85	  helper as the first prover objective). This is the Mathlib-idiomatic route:
86	  use the *concrete* `restrictFunctor` sectionwise API, not abstract monoidal
87	  functor structure.
88	
89	### Decision B: Route (B) — add `IsLocallyTrivial` and glue local isos
90	
91	- **Mathlib idiom**: there is **no `SheafOfModules` gluing primitive** for
92	  assembling local isos into a global iso. The idiomatic move is the reverse:
93	  build a *global canonical morphism* first, then check it is an iso *locally*
94	  (`isIso_iff_isIso_app` per open, or `isIso_iff_stalkFunctor_map_iso` per
95	  stalk). "Build locally then glue the morphism" forces a cocycle/overlap
96	  agreement check with no off-the-shelf support.
97	- **Project's path (proposed)**: add `(hM hN : IsLocallyTrivial …)`, trivialise
98	  on a common affine `W`, build the iso there, glue.
99	- **Gap**: divergent-with-cost on two counts. (1) The hypotheses are
100	  **mathematically unnecessary** — the lemma holds for arbitrary `M, N`
101	  (tensor commutes with open restriction), so adding them is a false constraint
102	  that future general consumers cannot satisfy. (2) Even with them, you still
103	  need a *global* comparison morphism to apply any iso criterion, so the
104	  triviality buys you nothing the global-morphism-checked-locally route (A)
105	  doesn't already give for free — and the "glue local isos" step is strictly
106	  *more* work than (A) (cocycle bookkeeping with no primitive).
107	- **Verdict**: **DIVERGE_INTENTIONALLY = do NOT take (B)**. It is heavier than
108	  (A) and over-constrains the signature. (Local triviality remains the right
109	  tool for the *consumers* — `exists_tensorObj_inverse`, the unit/associator
110	  isos — just not for `tensorObj_restrict_iso` itself.)
111	
112	### Decision δ: the abandoned mate route — `(PresheafOfModules.pullback φ).Monoidal`
113	
114	- **Mathlib idiom**: the δ map exists (`Adjunction.leftAdjointOplaxMonoidal`,
115	  Functor.lean:1009, per `mate207.md`), but its hypothesis
116	  `(pushforward φ).LaxMonoidal` reduces to lifting
117	  `ModuleCat.extendScalars`-monoidality to the presheaf level for a **general**
118	  ring map — a multi-file genuine monoidal-functor build.
119	- **Gap**: NEEDS_MATHLIB_GAP_FILL — multi-file. The iter-207
120	  `restrictScalarsLaxMonoidal` instance (now in the file, lines 105-178) was the
121	  partial down-payment; completing it for `pullback` is the wall.
122	- **Verdict**: **NEEDS_MATHLIB_GAP_FILL — correctly abandoned.** It modeled
123	  open restriction as a general base change, paying for monoidality the
124	  open-immersion case gets for free.
125	
126	## Recommendation
127	
128	Re-route Lane TS to **Route (A)** and do **not** pause TS — this iso does **not**
129	bottom out in absent multi-file Mathlib infra. The route is:
130	
131	1. **(Prerequisite, first prover objective, ~30–60 LOC.)** Build a sectionwise
132	   handle for `PresheafOfModules.pullback φ.hom` along the open-immersion ring
133	   map `φ = (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom f).hom` — i.e. a presheaf-level
134	   identification of the abstract pullback with the concrete
135	   reindex-along-`f.opensFunctor` + base-change-along-`appIso`, mirroring
136	   `restrictFunctorIsoPullback` one layer down. (Or reuse/adapt the unfolding
137	   helper the kaehler lane built — `analogies/kaehler-tensorequiv-presheafpullback.md`
138	   Decision 5.) This is the single genuine gap; it is bounded and single-file.
139	2. **(Main body.)** Keep the existing steps 1-2 (`restrictFunctorIsoPullback` +
140	   `sheafificationCompPullback`) reducing to the presheaf residual
141	   `(pullback φ.hom).obj (M.val ⊗ N.val) ≅ (M.restrict f).val ⊗ (N.restrict f).val`.
142	   Discharge it sectionwise via `PresheafOfModules.isoMk`: over each `U` both
143	   sides are `M(f''U) ⊗ N(f''U)` (by `restrict_obj` `rfl`), differing only by the
144	   scalar ring `O_X(U)` vs `O_Y(f''U)`, identified by
145	   `restrictScalarsEquivalenceOfRingEquiv (f.appIso U).commRingCatIsoToRingEquiv`.
146	   No `extendScalars` monoidality, no `(pullback φ).Monoidal`.
147	3. **(Iso check, if a morphism-then-iso shape is used instead of `isoMk`.)**
148	   `Hom.isIso_iff_isIso_app` (per-open) or `isIso_iff_stalkFunctor_map_iso`
149	   + `restrictStalkNatIso` (per-stalk).
150	
151	Drop Route (B): the lemma is true for arbitrary `M, N`, so do not add
152	`IsLocallyTrivial` hypotheses, and there is no Mathlib SheafOfModules
153	iso-gluing primitive to make "glue local isos" cheaper than (A). Keep the
154	iter-207 `restrictScalarsLaxMonoidal` instance only if some other consumer uses
155	it; for `tensorObj_restrict_iso` it is dead weight.
156	
157	**Blunt closability verdict: YES, closable by a `prove`-mode round on Route (A)
158	— conditional on first landing the bounded (~30–60 LOC) presheaf-pullback-
159	along-open-immersion sectionwise-unfolding helper.** It does NOT also bottom out
160	in absent multi-file monoidal infra. Sequence it as: helper objective → main

49	## Signature audit (iter-135 scaffold sign-off)
50	
51	Blueprint claim (`RigidityKbar.tex:464–468`): "view `G ×_k G` as a
52	`G`-scheme via `pr_1`. Then `Ω_{(G ×_k G)/G} ≅ pr_2^* Ω_{G/k}` as
53	presheaves of modules on `G ×_k G`."
54	
55	The Lean signature:
56	
57	- LHS `relativeDifferentialsPresheaf (fst G G).left` — uses `pr_1.left =
58	  (fst G G).left : (G ⊗ G).left ⟶ G.left` as the source morphism for
59	  relative differentials. Both `(fst G G).left` (and its target `G.left`)
60	  inhabit `Scheme.{u}`, so the LHS lives in `(G ⊗ G).left.PresheafOfModules`
61	  and exactly realises "Ω of `G ⊗ G` viewed as a `G`-scheme via `pr_1`". ✓
62	- RHS `(PresheafOfModules.pullback (toRingCatSheafHom (snd G G).left).hom)
63	  .obj (relativeDifferentialsPresheaf G.hom)` — starts from
64	  `relativeDifferentialsPresheaf G.hom : G.left.PresheafOfModules`
65	  (the cotangent of `G/Spec k`), pulls back along
66	  `pr_2.left = (snd G G).left : (G ⊗ G).left ⟶ G.left`. Lands in
67	  `(G ⊗ G).left.PresheafOfModules`. ✓
68	- φ shape `(Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom _).hom` is the canonical Mathlib
69	  φ for `PresheafOfModules.pullback` per iter-135 verdict
70	  (`Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Modules.Presheaf:42–45`). ✓

73	  signature would invalidate Step 3.

75	**Verdict: signature is correct — proceed.** The two sides agree on the
76	target presheaf, the asymmetry in `fst` vs `snd` correctly encodes the
77	blueprint asymmetry (LHS structure morphism vs RHS pullback morphism),
78	and Step 3's iter-136 closure already consumes this exact shape.

80	## Decisions identified
81	
82	### Decision 1: Mathlib idiom for sheaf-level base-change-of-Ω
83	
84	- **Mathlib idiom**: NONE PACKAGED. Search of Mathlib via
85	  `lean_leansearch` ("PresheafOfModules pullback differentials",
86	  "differentials tensor product presheaf") and `lean_loogle`
87	  (`PresheafOfModules.pullback _ ≅ _`) yields only
88	  `PresheafOfModules.pullbackId` and `PresheafOfModules.pullbackComp`,
89	  not a base-change-of-Ω lemma. The chart-level
90	  `KaehlerDifferential.tensorKaehlerEquiv`
91	  (`Mathlib/RingTheory/Kaehler/TensorProduct.lean:249`,
92	  signature `B ⊗[A] Ω[A⁄R] ≃ₗ[B] Ω[B⁄S]` under `Algebra.IsPushout R S A B`)
93	  is the closest pre-built artefact, and it has no sheaf-level
94	  promotion in Mathlib. Per `analogies/mulright-globalises-cotangent.md`
95	  Decision 2, Mathlib has no scheme-level relative cotangent **sheaf**
96	  at all — only the chart-level Kähler module and the project's
97	  home-grown `Scheme.relativeDifferentialsPresheaf` (built on
98	  `PresheafOfModules.DifferentialsConstruction.relativeDifferentials'`,
99	  `Mathlib/Algebra/Category/ModuleCat/Differentials/Presheaf.lean:189`).
100	
101	- **Project's path**: build the iso by hand. Chain
102	  `KaehlerDifferential.tensorKaehlerEquiv` (chart-level value identity)
103	  with `PresheafOfModules.isoMk`
104	  (`Mathlib/Algebra/Category/ModuleCat/Presheaf.lean:118`, the
105	  Mathlib idiom for assembling a chart-wise `app + naturality` pair
106	  into a `PresheafOfModules` iso) to deliver the natural iso.
107	
108	- **Gap**: NEEDS_MATHLIB_GAP_FILL — divergence is mandatory, Mathlib
109	  has the gap.
110	
111	- **Verdict**: **NEEDS_MATHLIB_GAP_FILL**, confirmed from iter-133. The
112	  project is correctly building the missing infrastructure.

114	### Decision 2: `Algebra.IsPushout`-from-affine-product source
115	
116	- **Mathlib idiom**: NO ONE-SHOT LEMMA. The chain available is:
117	  - `CommRingCat.isPushout_iff_isPushout`
118	    (`Mathlib/Algebra/Category/Ring/Constructions.lean:133`): bridges
119	    `CategoryTheory.IsPushout` in `CommRingCat` with `Algebra.IsPushout`.
120	  - `AlgebraicGeometry.isPullback_SpecMap_of_isPushout`
121	    (`Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/Pullbacks.lean:771`): gives a pullback
122	    in `Scheme` from a pushout in `CommRingCat`.
123	  - `AlgebraicGeometry.pullbackSpecIso`
124	    (`Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/Pullbacks.lean:703`):
125	    `Spec (S ⊗[R] T) ≅ Spec S ×_{Spec R} Spec T`.
126	  These give the chart-level `Algebra.IsPushout k Γ(G,V_1) Γ(G,V_2)
127	  Γ(G⊗G, W)` when `V_1, V_2` are affine charts of `G` over `Spec k` and
128	  `W = V_1 ×_{Spec k} V_2` is the affine chart of `G ⊗ G` cut out from
129	  them. But this only works on affine subcovers — not on the whole
130	  presheaf — and is not packaged.
131	
132	- **Project's path**: must build the `Algebra.IsPushout` chart-level
133	  lemma by hand from the universal property of binary products in
134	  `Over (Spec k)`. Envelope: ~80–150 LOC if the affine-chart
135	  combinatorics can be discharged uniformly via `pullbackSpecIso`;
136	  more if affine-cover bookkeeping bleeds into the closure.
137	
138	- **Gap**: NEEDS_MATHLIB_GAP_FILL — the chart-level `Algebra.IsPushout`
139	  helper does not exist and must be built.
140	
141	- **Verdict**: **NEEDS_MATHLIB_GAP_FILL**.

143	### Decision 3: Naturality assembly idiom
144	
145	- **Mathlib idiom**: `PresheafOfModules.isoMk`
146	  (`Mathlib/Algebra/Category/ModuleCat/Presheaf.lean:118`):
147	  ```
148	  def isoMk (app : ∀ (X : Cᵒᵖ), M₁.obj X ≅ M₂.obj X)
149	      (naturality : ∀ ⦃X Y : Cᵒᵖ⦄ (f : X ⟶ Y),
150	        M₁.map f ≫ (ModuleCat.restrictScalars (R.map f).hom).map (app Y).hom =
151	          (app X).hom ≫ M₂.map f := by cat_disch) : M₁ ≅ M₂
152	  ```
153	  The auto-generated simp lemmas `isoMk_hom_app`, `isoMk_inv_app` make
154	  the constructed iso amenable to downstream `simp`.
155	
156	- **Project's path**: use `isoMk`. For each open `V ⊆ (G ⊗ G).left`, the
157	  `app V` component is the composition
158	
159	  1. `relativeDifferentialsPresheaf_obj_kaehler` (`Differentials.lean:60`)
160	     identifies LHS sections over `V` as `Ω[Γ((G⊗G).left, V) ⁄
161	     ((fst G G).left⁻¹ O_{G.left})(V)]`.

163	     `Algebra.IsPushout`) gives a `Γ((G⊗G).left, V)`-linear iso
164	     `Γ((G⊗G).left, V) ⊗_{Γ(G.left, pr_2(V))} Ω[Γ(G.left, pr_2(V)) ⁄ k]
165	       ≃ Ω[Γ((G⊗G).left, V) ⁄ Γ(G.left, pr_1(V))]`.
166	  3. The RHS pulled-back presheaf is, by `PresheafOfModules.pullback`'s
167	     left-adjoint definition, the universal `Γ((G⊗G).left, V)`-module
168	     receiving `Ω[Γ(G.left, pr_2(V)) ⁄ k]` after restricting scalars
169	     along `(toRingCatSheafHom pr_2.left).hom.app V`. For affine V,
170	     this is exactly the tensor product on the LHS of step 2.
171	  - For non-affine V, the same identification holds by colimit-of-
172	    affine-charts naturality (this is where the work concentrates —
173	    presheaf vs sheaf, see Decision 4).
174	
175	- **Gap**: ALIGN_WITH_MATHLIB on the iso constructor (use `isoMk`).
176	  The naturality square then reduces to commutativity of the
177	  `tensorKaehlerEquiv` value identity with the restriction-of-Kähler-
178	  modules map, which is `KaehlerDifferential.tensorKaehlerEquiv_tmul_D`
179	  (`Mathlib/RingTheory/Kaehler/TensorProduct.lean:283`) applied
180	  pointwise — itself a finite check.

182	- **Verdict**: **ALIGN_WITH_MATHLIB**. Use `PresheafOfModules.isoMk`,
183	  not a hand-built `Iso.mk { hom, inv, ... }`.
184	
185	### Decision 4: `presheaf` vs `sheaf` and the non-affine-V hazard
186	
187	This is a CRITICAL observation the iter-133 verdict did not surface
188	explicitly.
189	
190	- The project's `Scheme.relativeDifferentialsPresheaf f` is built on
191	  `PresheafOfModules.DifferentialsConstruction.relativeDifferentials'`,
192	  which over each open `V` (affine or not) gives sections
193	  `Ω[Γ(X,V) ⁄ (f⁻¹ O_S)(V)]` — i.e. Kähler differentials of an
194	  algebra map that, for non-affine V, has no particular
195	  geometric interpretation. The presheaf is `relativeDifferentialsPresheaf`
196	  not `relativeDifferentialsSheaf`; there is no sheafification.
197	
198	- For affine V, both sides are computable Kähler modules of the
199	  natural algebra map (and `tensorKaehlerEquiv` connects them).
200	  For non-affine V, both sides are still defined (as Kähler modules
201	  of the corresponding non-affine ring map), but
202	  `tensorKaehlerEquiv`'s hypothesis (`Algebra.IsPushout`) is not
203	  generally satisfied on non-affine opens — affine pushout-of-rings
204	  is a property special to spectra, not arbitrary opens.
205	
206	- **Implication**: the chart-wise `app` of `isoMk` cannot just be
207	  "`tensorKaehlerEquiv` of the V-sections"; it must be derived
208	  from the universal property of Kähler differentials at the
209	  presheaf level. Concretely: define a `Derivation` of the LHS
210	  ring map into the RHS module at the presheaf level (one
211	  derivation per open V, plus naturality across V → W restrictions),
212	  invoke `KaehlerDifferential.lift` to produce the LHS-to-RHS map,
213	  and analogously construct the inverse. This is the same strategy
214	  Mathlib uses to construct `tensorKaehlerEquiv` itself
215	  (`Kaehler/TensorProduct.lean:225–283`), lifted from a ring to a
216	  presheaf of rings.
217	
218	- The alternative (affine-cover-and-glue + sheafification) requires
219	  the project to either (i) sheafify `relativeDifferentialsPresheaf`
220	  (which is itself a non-trivial separate piece of infrastructure)
221	  or (ii) restrict to the affine-open subcategory before applying
222	  `isoMk`. Both are heavier than the universal-property-at-presheaf
223	  approach.
224	
225	- **Gap**: divergent-with-cost. The "chart-wise + glue" mental model
226	  (which the blueprint at `RigidityKbar.tex:474–478` hints at) is
227	  not directly executable on `relativeDifferentialsPresheaf` since
228	  the presheaf hasn't been sheafified.
229	
230	- **Verdict**: **PROCEED** with the universal-property-at-presheaf
231	  approach; the chart-affine-cover narrative in the blueprint is
232	  informal motivation, not the actual Lean proof recipe.
233	
234	### Decision 5: LOC envelope refinement
235	
236	- iter-133 envelope: **~150–300 LOC**.
237	- Findings that pressure the envelope upward:
238	  - `PresheafOfModules.pullback` has no chart-wise unfolding lemma
239	    in Mathlib (`pullback` is `(pushforward φ).leftAdjoint`, opaque

242	    (likely via the pushforward-adjunction unit/counit unfolding).
243	  - No `Algebra.IsPushout`-from-affine-product helper (Decision 2):
244	    ~80–150 LOC chart-level helper.
245	  - Universal-property-at-presheaf-level naturality (Decision 4):
246	    ~100–200 LOC for the derivation construction + `lift` invocation +
247	    `tensorKaehlerEquiv`-style inverse + verification.
248	  - The `isoMk`-level naturality square: ~30–80 LOC.
249	- **Revised envelope: ~250–500 LOC** (upper bound widened by ~200 LOC
250	  vs iter-133). If the project decides to factor out the
251	  `PresheafOfModules.pullback`-unfolding helper as a stand-alone
252	  reusable lemma (recommended), the core proof envelope tightens
253	  to ~150–300 LOC and the helper adds ~50 LOC separately.
254	- **Verdict**: refined envelope **~250–500 LOC** for the body alone;
255	  ~300–550 LOC if the pullback-unfolding helper is co-shipped
256	  in the same file.

258	## Recommendation
259	
260	**Verdict on iter-135 scaffold signature: PROCEED — signature is
261	correct.** The `(fst G G).left` / `(snd G G).left` asymmetry between
262	LHS structure morphism and RHS pullback morphism correctly encodes
263	the blueprint claim. The φ-compatibility shape
264	`(Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom _).hom` matches iter-135's verdict
265	and Step 3's iter-136 closure (no convention drift).
266	
267	**Closure recipe (5-step prose outline)**:
268	
269	1. **Chart-level `Algebra.IsPushout`** (~80–150 LOC):
270	   Build a helper lemma — for affine `V_1 ⊆ G.left`, `V_2 ⊆ G.left`
271	   over a common affine `U ⊆ Spec k`, with `W = V_1 ×_{U} V_2 ⊆
272	   (G ⊗ G).left` (the affine chart of `G ⊗ G` cut out via
273	   `pullbackSpecIso`), conclude
274	   `Algebra.IsPushout Γ(Spec k, U) Γ(G.left, V_1) Γ(G.left, V_2)
275	     Γ((G ⊗ G).left, W)`.
276	   Uses `CommRingCat.isPushout_iff_isPushout`
277	   (Constructions.lean:133), `pullbackSpecIso` (Pullbacks.lean:703),
278	   `isPullback_SpecMap_of_isPushout` (Pullbacks.lean:771).
279	   (Stand-alone reusable lemma — file in `Differentials.lean` or
280	   a new utility section in `Cotangent/GrpObj.lean`.)
281	
282	2. **`PresheafOfModules.pullback` chart-level unfolding** (~30–60 LOC):
283	   Build a helper unfolding
284	   `((PresheafOfModules.pullback φ).obj M).presheaf.obj V ≃
285	     Γ(R, V) ⊗_{((Opens.map F).op ⋙ S).obj V} M.presheaf.obj (F.op.obj V)`
286	   for a `PresheafOfModules` `M`. Derive from the left-adjoint definition
287	   of `pullback` (Pullback.lean:44–45) and the
288	   `pullbackPushforwardAdjunction` counit/unit
289	   (Pullback.lean:50). This is the load-bearing infrastructure
290	   gap caught in Decision 4 + general-purpose agent's finding 4.
291	
292	3. **Chart-wise derivation `D_V`** (~50–80 LOC):
293	   For each open `V ⊆ (G ⊗ G).left`, define an `S_1`-derivation
294	   `D_V : Γ((G ⊗ G).left, V) → (pullback presheaf).obj V`
295	   where `S_1 = ((Opens.map pr_1.base).op ⋙ G.left.presheaf).obj V`.
296	   Concretely, `D_V(b)` is the image of `1 ⊗ d_{pr_2(V)}(b)` under
297	   the `tensorKaehlerEquiv` value identity composed with the
298	   helper from Step 2.
299	
300	4. **Apply `KaehlerDifferential.lift`** (~30–50 LOC):
301	   On each V, the universal property of Kähler differentials gives
302	   a unique `Γ((G⊗G).left, V)`-linear map
303	   `Ω[Γ((G⊗G).left, V) ⁄ S_1(V)] → (pullback presheaf).obj V`
304	   extending `D_V`. By naturality of `lift`, these glue into a
305	   morphism of presheaves of modules `LHS → RHS`.
306	
307	5. **Inverse + `PresheafOfModules.isoMk` assembly** (~80–150 LOC):
308	   Construct the inverse the same way (`tensorKaehlerEquiv` is
309	   computable from `D_S`, so the inverse `D'_V` is defined by
310	   sending `b ⊗ ω` to the universal-derivation image). Invoke
311	   `PresheafOfModules.isoMk` (Presheaf.lean:118) to package the

852	  well-defined on isomorphism classes: an isomorphism \(L \cong L_0\) tensors
853	  to an isomorphism \(L \otimes L' \cong L_0 \otimes L'\) by functoriality of
854	  \(\otimes\) (\cref{lem:scheme_modules_tensorobj_functoriality}), and
855	  symmetrically in the second argument. The group axioms are then
856	  \emph{exactly} the four existence-of-isomorphism facts established above,
857	  read on isomorphism classes:
858	  \begin{itemize}
859	    \item \textbf{associativity} \(([L]+[M])+[N] = [L]+([M]+[N])\) is
860	      \cref{lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso};
861	    \item \textbf{neutral element} \([\mathcal{O}_{C\times_k T}] + [L] = [L]\)
862	      (and on the right) is \cref{lem:tensorobj_unit_iso};
863	    \item \textbf{commutativity} \([L]+[M]=[M]+[L]\) is
864	      \cref{lem:tensorobj_comm_iso};
865	    \item \textbf{inverse} \([L] + [L^{-1}] = [\mathcal{O}_{C\times_k T}]\),
866	      with \(L^{-1}=\mathcal{H}om(L,\mathcal{O}_{C\times_k T})\), is
867	      \cref{lem:tensorobj_inverse_invertible}.
868	  \end{itemize}
869	  Each axiom is a \emph{proposition} --- the existence of an isomorphism ---
870	  so no monoidal-coherence datum (pentagon, triangle, hexagon) is invoked or
871	  required; the \(\AddCommGroup\) on \(\Pic(C \times_k T)\) is assembled
872	  directly from these four \(\mathrm{Nonempty}(\cdots \cong \cdots)\)
873	  statements. This mirrors Mathlib's own Picard group:
874	  \(\mathtt{CommRing.Pic}\,R\) carries its \(\mathtt{CommGroup}\)
875	  (\(\mathtt{instCommGroupPic}\), \texttt{Mathlib.RingTheory.PicardGroup})
876	  transported from
877	  \(\mathrm{Units}\bigl(\mathrm{Skeleton}(\mathtt{ModuleCat}\,R)\bigr)\), the
878	  iso-class arithmetic being the family of existence-of-iso facts
879	  \([M \otimes N] = [M]\cdot[N]\) and \([M^{\vee}] = [M]^{-1}\); here the same
880	  shape is transported to the scheme-level line-bundle subcategory
881	  \(\texttt{LineBundle.OnProduct}\,\pi_C\,\pi_T\). (The scheme-level Picard
882	  group is genuinely absent from Mathlib --- \texttt{Mathlib.RingTheory.PicardGroup}
883	  records ``connect to invertible sheaves'' as an open task --- so the project
884	  supplies it lightly.) The invertibility-predicate carrier is the project's
885	  \(\mathtt{IsLocallyTrivial}\), mirroring Mathlib's \(\mathtt{Module.Invertible}\)
886	  verbatim.
887	
888	  By \cref{lem:pullback_compatible_with_tensorobj}, the pullback functor
889	  \(\pi_T^{*} : \texttt{LineBundle}\,T \to
890	   \texttt{LineBundle.OnProduct}\,\pi_C\,\pi_T\) carries tensor products to
891	  tensor products and the unit to the unit, so the induced map on isomorphism
892	  classes \(\pi_T^{*} : \Pic(T) \to \Pic(C \times_k T)\) is a group
893	  homomorphism, and its image is a subgroup.
894	
895	  By \cref{thm:relative_pic_quotient_well_defined}, the underlying set of
896	  \(\Pic^{\sharp}_{C/k}(T)\) is the set of cosets of this subgroup. The
897	  quotient of an abelian group by a subgroup is itself an abelian group, via
898	  the standard \(\mathtt{QuotientAddGroup}\) construction, and the quotient
899	  map is a surjective group homomorphism whose kernel is exactly the subgroup
900	  (Stacks tag 01CR for the Picard-group special case; the general
901	  quotient-of-abelian-groups statement is in any algebra textbook). Putting
902	  the pieces together gives the \(\AddCommGroup\) instance on
903	  \(\Pic^{\sharp}_{C/k}(T)\) of the statement, and identifies it with the
904	  structure of \cref{lem:rel_pic_sharp_groupoid}.
905	\end{proof}

1	# `tensorObj_restrict_iso` — precise missing ingredient (iter-206 Lane TS)
2	
3	## Target
4	
5	```lean
6	noncomputable def tensorObj_restrict_iso {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : Y ⟶ X)
7	    [IsOpenImmersion f] (M N : X.Modules) :
8	    (tensorObj M N).restrict f ≅ tensorObj (M.restrict f) (N.restrict f)
9	```
10	where `tensorObj M N := (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).obj
11	(PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj M.val N.val)`.
12	
13	## Progress this iter (in the file, compiles GREEN)
14	
15	The proof now performs **two real reduction steps** before the residual `sorry`:
16	
17	1. `Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctorIsoPullback f` — reduces `· .restrict f` to
18	   `(pullback f).obj ·`. (Mathlib, `AlgebraicGeometry.Modules.Sheaf`.)
19	2. `SheafOfModules.sheafificationCompPullback f.toRingCatSheafHom` — **the key
20	   lemma surfaced this iter**: `sheafification ⋙ pullback φ ≅
21	   PresheafOfModules.pullback φ.hom ⋙ sheafification`
22	   (`Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Sheaf.PullbackContinuous`). Moves the
23	   pullback *inside* the sheafification. This **discharges half (ii)** of the
24	   obstruction recorded in earlier iters ("sheafification commutes with
25	   pullback") — it IS in Mathlib, contrary to the old docstring claim.
26	
27	After these the goal is the **purely presheaf-level** residual
28	```
29	sheafify ((PresheafOfModules.pullback φ.hom).obj (M.val ⊗ N.val))
30	  ≅ (M.restrict f).tensorObj (N.restrict f)
31	```
32	
33	## The single precise missing ingredient
34	
35	A **`Monoidal` (or at least oplax-monoidal-with-iso-comparison) instance for
36	`PresheafOfModules.pullback φ`** w.r.t. `PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj`,
37	i.e. the base-change isomorphism
38	```
39	(PresheafOfModules.pullback φ.hom).obj (A ⊗ B)
40	   ≅ (PresheafOfModules.pullback φ.hom).obj A ⊗ (PresheafOfModules.pullback φ.hom).obj B.
41	```
42	
43	### Why flatness does NOT shortcut this (correction to the iter-206 plan framing)
44	
45	The plan's "re-scope to line bundles ⇒ flat-exactness closes it" addresses only
46	*iso-ness* of a comparison map. But `PresheafOfModules.pullback φ` is defined as
47	an **abstract left adjoint** `(pushforward φ).leftAdjoint`
48	(`Mathlib/.../Presheaf/Pullback.lean:44`) — there is **no sectionwise formula**,
49	so there is no canonical comparison map to even state, let alone prove iso. The
50	comparison map is the **oplax-monoidal structure of the left adjoint of the
51	(strong) monoidal `pushforward`** (doctrinal/mate of `pushforward`'s lax
52	structure). That oplax structure is what is absent from Mathlib; flatness would
53	only upgrade it from a map to an iso *after* it exists. So re-scoping the
54	signature to line bundles is **not** sufficient to unblock — the blocker is
55	upstream of the flatness step.
56	
57	### What IS present in Mathlib (so the gap is "lift", not "invent")
58	
59	- `ModuleCat.extendScalars` **is** `Monoidal`
60	  (`Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Monoidal.Adjunction:42`; `μ`/`ε` via
61	  `AlgebraTensorModule` base-change isos). This is the *sectionwise* statement.
62	- `PresheafOfModules.pushforward₀OfCommRingCat` **is** `Monoidal`
63	  (`.../Presheaf/PushforwardZeroMonoidal.lean:33`) — but only because reindexing
64	  has `μIso = Iso.refl`; the extension-of-scalars lift is genuine work.
65	- `SheafOfModules.sheafificationCompPullback`, `pullbackIso`,
66	  `Scheme.Modules.Hom.isIso_iff_isIso_app`, `hom_ext` (gluing) — all present.
67	
68	### Suggested route for a future `mathlib-build` lane
69	
70	Build, project-side, a `(PresheafOfModules.pullback φ).Monoidal` (or an explicit
71	`PresheafOfModules.pullbackMonoidalComparison` iso) by **lifting
72	`ModuleCat.extendScalars.Monoidal` sectionwise** through the
73	partial-left-adjoint construction, OR by the doctrinal-adjunction route
74	(left adjoint of strong-monoidal `pushforward` is oplax; strong because each
75	section is the `extendScalars` strong-monoidal comparison). Then
76	`tensorObj_restrict_iso` closes as
77	`sheafification.mapIso (pullbackMonoidalComparison …) ≪≫ (units bookkeeping)`,
78	where the units bookkeeping relates `sheafify (pullback M.val)` to
79	`M.restrict f` via `restrictFunctorIsoPullback`/`pullbackIso`.
80	
81	This is the same *class* of multi-file Mathlib gap as the abandoned
82	`MonoidalClosed (PresheafOfModules R₀)` route, but **strictly smaller**: the
83	sectionwise ingredient already exists, and the sheafification half is now
84	discharged by `sheafificationCompPullback`.
85	
86	## Downstream (all blocked on the above)
87	
88	`tensorObj_assoc_iso`, `tensorObj_comm_iso`, the arbitrary-`L` unit iso,
89	`exists_tensorObj_inverse`, and `addCommGroup_via_tensorObj` all consume
90	`tensorObj_restrict_iso` (global associator/unitor/braiding need it on a common
91	trivialising cover, glued via `isIso_iff_isIso_app`). They cannot close until
92	the base-change instance lands.
93
